# @andes/textfield

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Text Field](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/258d8b-textfield/b/26f535)_.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

You'll also need to install `@andes/form`, which will provide styling.

```bash
npm install --save @andes/textfield @andes/form
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle. You'll also need to import the Form component, if you weren't already:

```scss
@import '@andes/textfield/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```scss
// Base
@import '@andes/textfield/base';

// By modifier
// Warning
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/modifier/warning';
// Indeterminate
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/base';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/indeterminate';
@import '@andes/progress-indicator-circular/lib/xsmall';
// Readonly
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/modifier/readonly';

// By size
// Medium
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/size/medium';

// CodeInput
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/code-input';

// Split
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/split';
@import '@andes/list/index';
@import '@andes/floating-menu/base';
@import '@andes/dropdown/base';
@import '@andes/dropdown/lib/styles/form';

// Password
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/password';

// Centered
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/centered';

// With preffix or suffix
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/preffix-suffix';

// With clear button
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/clear';

// With countdown
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/countdown';

// With embedded component like tooltip, button or checkbox
@import '@andes/textfield/lib/styles/embedded';
```

> Always import `@andes/textfield/base` and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { TextField, CodeInput, Password, SplitTextfield } from '@andes/textfield';
```

Please use `TextField` as children of `Form` component, for easier handling.

## Examples

### TextField

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Text Field](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/textfield-textfield--andes-text-field).

```jsx
<TextField label="Phone number" helper="Ej. (11) 4790-533" placeholder="Enter phone number />
```

### Textfield with Tooltip

To improve accesibility the trigger should always be an interactive element such as a `button`.

```jsx
<TextField id="demo-textfield-with-tooltip" label="Label" placeholder="Placeholder" helper="Some hint">
  <Tooltip
    content="This means everything is alright! This means everything is alright! This means everything is alright!"
    title="Success!"
  >
    <Button aria-label="Más información" hierarchy="mute" size="small">
      <Helper16 color="#3483fa" />
    </Button>
  </Tooltip>
</TextField>
```

### SplitTextfield

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Split](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/textfield-splittextfield--andes-split-text-field).

```jsx
const splitListItems = [
  { title: 'Opción 1' },
  { title: 'Opción 2' },
  { title: 'Opción 3' },
  { title: 'Opción 4' },
];

return (
  <SplitTextfield
    className="demo-split-textfield"
    label="Main label"
    placeholder="Placeholder"
    list={
      onClick: null,
      content: splitListItems,
    }
  />
);
```

### CodeInput

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Code Input](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/textfield-codeinput--andes-code-input).

```jsx
<CodeInput
  id="code-input-test"
  label="Personal ID"
  digits={4}
  helper="Some hint to help the user"
/>
```

### Password

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Password](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?selectedKind=TextField&selectedStory=Password).

```jsx
<Password
  className="demo-textfield"
  label="Clave"
  placeholder="Placeholder"
/>
```

To apply show password feature:

```jsx
<Password
  className="demo-textfield"
  label="Clave"
  placeholder="Placeholder"
  showHide
/>
```

### Textfield/SplitTextfield With Masks

```jsx
import { PHONE } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/ar';
import useMask from '@andes/textfield/build/hooks/useMask';

const TextfieldWithMask = (props) => {
  const textfieldRef = useRef();
  const maskOptions = PHONE;

  useMask(textfieldRef, { maskOptions, ...props });

  return (
    <TextField
      ref={textfieldRef}
      {...otherProps}
    />
  );
};

const SplitTextfieldWithMask = (props) => {
  const splitTextfieldRef = useRef();
  const maskOptions = PHONE;

  useMask(splitTextfieldRef, { maskOptions, ...props });

  return (
    <SplitTextfield
      ref={splitTextfieldRef}
      {...otherProps}
    />
  );
};
```

### Shadow DOM
Usage inside an isolated component with Shadow DOM. See [@andes/shadow-dom](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/blob/master/packages/shadow-dom/README.md) package

```jsx
import { useEnableAndesShadowDOM } from '@andes/shadow-dom';

useEnableAndesShadowDOM();

const splitListItems = [
  { title: 'Opción 1' },
  { title: 'Opción 2' },
  { title: 'Opción 3' },
  { title: 'Opción 4' },
];

return (
  <SplitTextfield
    className="demo-split-textfield"
    label="Main label"
    placeholder="Placeholder"
    list={
      onClick: null,
      content: splitListItems,
    }
  />
);
```

#### Masks

For convenience we have bundled a few masks, you can take a look at them in @andes/textfield/masks, each locale contains a custom set of masks ready to be used, currently we ship:

| AR | BR | CL | CO | EC | MX | PE | UY | VE |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| date | date | --- | --- | --- | date | --- | --- | --- |
| datetime | datetime | --- | --- | --- | datetime | --- | --- | --- |
| money | money | money | money | --- | money | --- | --- | --- |
| phone | phone | --- | --- | --- | PHONE | --- | --- | --- |
| zipcode | zipcode | --- | --- | --- | ZIPCODE | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | CNPJ | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | CPF | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | mobile | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | RG | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| DNI | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CUIL | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | RFC | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- | NIT | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | RUC | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | RUC | --- | --- |
| --- | --- | RUT | --- | --- | --- | --- | RUT | --- |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | RIF |

To use any mask you need to import it in your project:

```jsx
import { date, datetime, money, PHONE, ZIPCODE, DNI, CUIL } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/ar';
import { date, datetime, money, PHONE, ZIPCODE, CNPJ, CPF, RG, mobile } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/br';
import { money, RUT } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/cl'
import { money, NIT } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/co'
import { RUC } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/ec'
import { date, datetime, money, PHONE, RFC, ZIPCODE } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/mx'
import { RUC } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/pe'
import { RUT } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/uy'
import { RIF } from '@andes/textfield/build/masks/ve'
```

In case you need other masks, it needs some specific structure as shown below:

```jsx
{
  mask: 'N-D', // string: mask to apply (REQUIRED)
  tokens: { // custom tokens object (REQUIRED)
    N: { pattern: /\d/, multiple: false }, // multiple option is used if the pattern should be applied to multiple characters before special character as '-' in this case
    D: { pattern: /\d/ },
  },
  preProcess: func(val), // function to pre process the input value before applying and formatting the mask
  postProcess: func(val), // function to post process the input value after applying and formatting the mask
  eager: bool, // default: false, eager mode will show static characters before you type them, e.g. when you type 1, eager mask #-# will show 1- and non-eager will show 1
};
```

## Props

### Textfield

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `autoFocus` | Whether the field should be focused automatically | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `centered` | Whether the field should be centered | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `children` | Custom children component, could be a `Checkbox`, `Button`, `Icon` or `Tooltip` component | `node` | `'undefined'`  |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `clear` |  Whether the field should display a clear button | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `countdown` | Whether the field should display a character counter. Best suited when set to `multiline`, and works with `maxLength` | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Default value for field (uncontrolled behavior) | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the field should be disabled. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `focus` | Whether to force the focus on textfield | `boolean` | `false` |
| `helper` | Text or node to display as helper to the user (or as error message) | `string, node` | `undefined` | |
| `id` | Component identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `inputProps` | [Attributes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#attributes) applied to the input element. | `object` | `{}` |  |
| `label` | Text to display as label | `string` | `'undefined'` |  |
| `maxLength` | Maximum number of characters permitted in the field. Used when `countdown` is set | `number\|string` | `120` |  |
| `modifier` | Modifier to apply different styles of the textfield |  `string: 'error', 'completed', 'indeterminate', 'readonly'` | `undefined`  |  |
| `multiline` | Whether the field should act as textarea | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `onBlur` | Callback that executes when `blur` event triggers | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onChange` | Callback that executes when the content of the input changes | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onFocus` | Callback that executes when `focus` event triggers | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onInput` | Callback that executes when the user interface on the input changes | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `placeholder` | Placeholder of the field | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `prefix` | Text to display as prefix | `string` | `''` |  |
| `rows` | Native `rows` attribute, used when `multiline` is set | `number\|string` | `1` |  |
| `select` | Whether to force the select over the textfield | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `size` | Size of the component | `string: 'large', 'medium'` | `large` |  |
| `suffix` | Text to display as suffix | `string` | `''` |  |
| `value` | Value for field (controlled behavior)| `number \| string` | `undefined` |  |
| `srLabel` | Input label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

> There are times when the information in our form is sensitive data or values that cannot be reused, in these cases you will need to disable the autocomplete of your form. By default this functionality is on, but you can turn it off by adding the autocomplete=”off” attribute to your form element. In case you use a type=password input and you don't want the value to autocomplete, you can add the autocomplete=”new-password” attribute to your input so that the field is not filled. For more information about autocomplete you can read the following links: [whatwg](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/form-control-infrastructure.html#autofill), [web.dev](https://web.dev/sign-in-form-best-practices/), [mdn](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Securing_your_site/Turning_off_form_autocompletion).

Example:

```jsx
<Textfield inputProps={{ autocomplete: 'new-password', type: 'password' }} />

<Password inputProps={{ autocomplete: 'new-password' }} />
```

### `<SplitTextfield>` Props extends `<Textfield>` [API](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-andes-ui/tree/master/packages/textfield#textfield)

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `autoFocus` | Whether the field should be focused automatically | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `children` | Custom children component, could be a `Text`, `Progress indicator` or `Tooltip` component | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `defaultSelection` | Index of the default element selected in the menu | `number` | `0` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Default value for field (uncontrolled behavior) | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `disabled` | Whether the field should be disabled. | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `helper` | Text or node to display as helper to the user (or as error message) | `string, node` | `undefined` | |
| `id` | Component identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `inputProps` | [Attributes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#attributes) applied to the input element. | `object` | `{}` |  |
| `label` | Text to display as label | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `list` | List of options to show inside the menu | `object<Items>` | `undefined` |  |
| `modifier` | Modifier to apply different styles of the texfield |  `string: 'error', 'completed', 'indeterminate', 'readonly'` | `undefined`  |  |
| `onBlur` | Callback that executes when `blur` event triggers | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onChange` | Callback that executes when the content of the input changes | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onDropdownOpen` | Callback executed when the dropdown is opened | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onFocus` | Callback that executes when `focus` event triggers | `function(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `placeholder` | Placeholder of the field | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `splitIcon` | Icon to show in the dropdown button | `node` | `undefined` |  |
| `splitLabel` | Label to show in the dropdown button | `string \| node` | `undefined` |  |
| `splitPosition` | Position according to the field to show the menu | `string: 'left', 'right'` | `'left'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Input label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `value` | Value for field (controlled behavior)| `number \| string` | `undefined` |  |

#### Items

```jsx
  {
    onClick: func, // Callback executed when item is selected 
    content: arrayOf(
      shape({
        title: string, // Text of the list item
        icon: func, // Icon to show next to the option if needed
      }),
    ),
  }
```

### CodeInput Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `autoFocus` | Whether the field should be focused automatically | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `centered` | Whether the fields and respective texts should be centered or positioned left | `boolean` | `false` | |
| `className` | Custom class name for the component | `string` | `''` | |
| `defaultValue` | Default value for field (uncontrolled behavior) | `string, arrayOf(string)` | `undefined` |  |
| `digits` | Number of digits | `number` | `1` | |
| `disabled` | Whether the field should be disabled | `boolean` | `false` | |
| `focus` | Number of the digit to focus, or if `true` is passed, the 1st element will be focused | `boolean, number` | `0` | |
| `helper` | Text or node to display as helper to the user (or as error message) | `string, node` | `undefined` | |
| `id` | Component identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `label` | Text to display as label | `string` | `'` | |
| `modifier` | Modifier to add the corresponding styling and behavior |  `string: 'error', 'completed'` | `undefined`  | |
| `onChange` | Callback that triggers when the value changes | `func(event, values)` | `undefined`  | |
| `onPaste` | Callback that triggers when a value is copied | `func(event, newValues)` | `undefined`  | |
| `srLabel` | Input label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `strict` | Whether the field should allow only numbers without any special characters | `boolean` | `false` | |
| `value` | Value for field (controlled mode) | `string, arrayOf(string)` | `undefined` | |

> ⚠️ You may run into scenarios where you can enter text into the <CodeInput> component in browsers like **Safari** and **Firefox**, even though you can see them on the screen, they are not valid values, therefore the browser generates an alert when submitting the form.

### Password Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `autoFocus` | Whether the field should be focused automatically | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `defaultValue` | Default value for field (uncontrolled behavior) | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `helper` | Text or node to display as helper to the user (or as error message) | `string, node` | `undefined` | |
| `id` | Component identifier | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `inputProps` | [Attributes](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#attributes) applied to the input element. | `object` | `{}` |  |
| `label` | Text to display as label | `string` | `'undefined'` |  |
| `maxLength` | Maximum number of characters permitted in the field. Used when `countdown` is set | `number` | `120` |  |
| `modifier` | Modifier for styles of the textfield |  `string: 'error', 'completed', 'indeterminate'` | `undefined`  |  |
| `onBlur` | Callback that executes when `blur` event triggers | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onChange` | Callback that executes when the content of the input changes | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `onClickShowPassword` | Callback that executes when show password button is clicked | `func(event)` | `undefined`  |  |
| `onFocus` | Callback that executes when `focus` event triggers | `func(event)` | `undefined` |  |
| `placeholder` | Placeholder of the field | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `showHide` | If `true` it enables the show/hide password feature | `boolean` | `false` |  |
| `srLabel` | Input label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `value` | Value for field (controlled behavior)| `number \| string` | `undefined` |  |

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

TextField allows modifying the following attributes:

| Attribute       | Description                           |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `fill`          | Textfield fill color.                 |
| `label`         | Textfield label color.                |
| `placeholder`   | Textfield placeholder color.          |
| `inputText`     | Textfield input text color.           |
| `preffix`       | Textfield prefix text color.          |
| `suffix`        | Textfield suffix text color.          |
| `text-checkbox` | Textfield checkbox label color.       |
| `tooltip`       | Textfield tooltip icon color.         |
| `validated`     | Textfield validated check icon color. |
| `clear`         | Textfield clear button icon color.    |
| `border`        | Textfield border color.               |

Taking this into account, the `customTheme` object has the following structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    textfield: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          [state]: fillColorValue,
        },
        label: {
          [state]: labelColorValue,
        },
        placeholder: {
          default: placeholderColorValue,
        },
        prefix: {
          [state]: prefixColorValue,
        },
        sufix: {
          [state]: suffixColorValue,
        },
        tooltip: {
          [state]: tooltipColorValue,
        },
        validated: validatedIconColorValue,
        clear: {
          [state]: tooltipColorValue,
        },
        border: {
          [state]: borderColorValue
        },
      },
    },
  }
};
```

Possible `state` values:

```jsx

state: default | hover | active | disabled | readOnly | error

```

> For a more detailed view of the structure of this object you can refer to the file `@andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json`

#### Example

<img src="resources/textfield/docs/Textfield-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Textfield Example" width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { TextField } from '@andes/textfield';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    textfield: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          default: 'yellow',
          readOnly: 'lightyellow',
        },
        label: {
          default: 'violet',
        },
        placeholder: {
          default: 'lightgray',
        },
        inputText: {
          default: 'blue',
        },
        validated: 'lightblue',
        clear: {
          default: 'orange',
          hover: 'cyan',
          active: 'green',
        },
        border: {
          default: 'lightblue',
          hover: 'blue',
          active: 'violet',
          error: 'magenta',
          disabled: 'yellow',
          readOnly: 'lightgray'
        }
      },
    }
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <TextField
      label="Label"
      placeholder="Placeholder"
      helper="Some hint to help the user"
      modifier="completed"
      clear
    />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/textfield/index';
@import '@andes/form/lib/styles/helper/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    textfield: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes TextField regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.
>

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

#### `TextField` data-attributes
| data-attribute                      | Description |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------- |
| data-andes-textfield                | TextField main container. |
| data-andes-textfield-modifier       | TextField modifier value. Can be: `completed`, `indeterminated`, `readonly`. |
| data-andes-textfield-label          | Textfield label container.  |
| data-andes-textfield-prefix         | TextField prefix container. |
| data-andes-textfield-input          | TextField input control. |
| data-andes-textfield-input-type     | TextField input control type. Can be: `input`, `textarea`  |
| data-andes-textfield-suffix         | TextField suffix container. |
| data-andes-textfield-right-content  | Textfield right slot container. |
| data-andes-textfield-bottom-info    | TextField bottom info container.  |
| data-andes-textfield-helper         | Textfield Helper. |
| data-andes-textfield-countdown      | Textfield countdown container.  |
| data-andes-state                    | TextField state. Can be `disabled`, `warning`, `error`. |

#### `CodeInput` data-attributes
| data-attribute                      | Description |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------- |
| data-andes-codeinput                | CodeInput main container. |
| data-andes-codeinput-modifier       | CodeInput modifier value. Can be, `completed` |
| data-andes-codeinput-label          | CodeInput label. |
| data-andes-codeinput-helper         | CodeInput input Helper. |
| data-andes-state                    | CodeInput state. Can be `disabled`, `error`. |
| data-andes-codeinput-input          | CodeInput input control. |

#### `Password` data-attributes
| data-attribute                    | Description |
| --------------------------------- | ----------- |
| data-andes-password               | Password textfield main container.  |
| data-andes-password-input         | Password textfield input control. |
| data-andes-password-helper        | Password textfield Helper. |

#### `SplitTextfield` data-attributes
| data-attribute                    | Description |
| --------------------------------- | ----------- |
| data-andes-split-textfield         | SplitTextfield main container.  |
| data-andes-split-textfield-helper  | SplitTextfield Helper. |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

#### Example

<img src="resources/textfield/docs/Textfield-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Textfield Example"  width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { TextField } from '@andes/textfield';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    textfield: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '[data-andes-textfield-label]': {
          'font-style': 'italic',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <TextField
      helper="Some hint to help the user"
      label="Label"
      placeholder="Placeholder"
    />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/textfield/index';
@import '@andes/form/lib/styles/helper/index';
```
